name: The Time Machine
primary_sources: The Time Machine by H.G. Wells
The Chronic Argonauts by H.G. Wells

distinctive_features: Illustrations depicting futuristic and dystopian landscapes
Depictions of the Eloi and Morlocks
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: approximately 20 cm x 13 cm x 2 cm

domain_specific_visual.cover_art: Victorian scientist with a time machine
domain_specific_visual.illustrations: scenes from the future and time travel

relationships.description: The Time Machine was developed from Wells's earlier work, The Chronic Argonauts.
The depiction of the Eloi was inspired by the utopic romance novel News from Nowhere.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: pioneered the concept of time travel in literature
primary_sources: The Time Machine by H.G. Wells
The Chronic Argonauts by H.G. Wells

contrastive_constraints: NOT a generic science fiction novel
NOT a modern time travel story
NOT a purely utopian or dystopian narrative
